page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 96 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv9 architecture (Neoverse-V2) running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity is a key feature, delivering a baseline bandwidth of 300 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, scoring in the top 10% for both small-block cached and large-block uncached read and write operations. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top 10%, alongside static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and gzip compression benchmarks falling in the middle 50% average tier.

Given its high core density and massive 300 Gbps network bandwidth, the m8gn.24xlarge is optimized for network-heavy, multi-threaded workloads. It is highly efficient for large-scale database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing. While it lacks hardware acceleration, it demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds for medium and large LLM inference workloads. The ARM64 architecture offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded enterprise applications, though workloads requiring legacy x86 instructions or local SSD storage will face compatibility and performance tradeoffs.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64-based general-purpose server instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM and a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this Nitro-based instance is optimized for network-intensive and highly parallelized workloads. Benchmark results place the instance in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance and cryptographic operations remain in the average tier. Lacking local storage and GPU hardware, the instance relies entirely on network-attached storage. It is highly suited for large-scale database deployments, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput web serving, offering strong qualitative cost efficiency for modern ARM-native enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64 server with 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge features 96 ARM64 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps bandwidth for high-throughput multi-core workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered instance delivering 96 dedicated physical cores and 300 Gbps network bandwidth for highly parallelized, network-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz on AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2.
384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio.
300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput data transfer.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis database benchmark performance.
No local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage.
Suited for large-scale databases, web serving, and multi-threaded software compilation.
